title: First Solar Hit by Manufacturing Issues, Terminated Contracts in Q3
text: First Solar experienced a 10.8% year-over-year revenue decrease in Q3 2024, dropping to $887 million. The company attributed this downturn to manufacturing difficulties and contract terminations. The Arizona-based solar manufacturer faced multiple operational challenges. A $50 million warranty expense resulted from module performance issues. The company resolved these by improving its glass cleaning process and correcting an error in predicting engineering performance margins. Several contracts were prematurely ended, including a 0.4 GW agreement with Plug Power and two contracts in India. Notably, termination payments actually boosted gross profit during the quarter. Some clients requested delivery timing shifts due to project development delays, forcing the company to either enforce warehouse shipments or accommodate schedule adjustments. First Solar faces competitive headwinds, including Chinese price dumping in the Indian market and broader industry volatility. The company reduced its full-year volume guidance by 1.2 GW to approximately 14.4 gigawatts. Despite current challenges, First Solar remains focused on expansion, with its Alabama facility operational since September and a Louisiana facility planned for H1 2025, positioning the company toward its goal of over 14 GW of U.S. annual capacity by 2026.
